intro.title: import statements
intro.bodyMarkdown:
`import math` loads a module; `from math import sqrt` imports names directly.

Modules organize code into reusable files. Python has a large standard library.

Avoid `from module import *` — it pollutes namespace and hides origins.
intro.sandboxCode:
import math
from random import randint
print(math.sqrt(16), randint(1, 6))

intro.title: Standard library overview
intro.bodyMarkdown:
The standard library includes os, sys, pathlib, json, datetime, math, random, re, and more — batteries included.

Check docs before pip installing — many needs are already covered.

Modules group related functionality; learn to browse documentation.

challenges.title: Medium: pathlib Path
challenges.starterCode:
from pathlib import Path
ok=Path(".") is not None

intro.title: math module
intro.bodyMarkdown:
`math` provides sqrt, sin, cos, floor, ceil, log, pi, e, and more for floating-point math.

Import math and call math.function(). For complex numbers use cmath.

Angles in radians unless you convert from degrees.

intro.title: random module
intro.bodyMarkdown:
`random` provides pseudorandom numbers: random(), randint, choice, shuffle, sample.

Not cryptographic — use secrets module for security tokens.

Seed with random.seed() for reproducible tests.

challenges.title: Hard: seed
challenges.starterCode:
import random
random.seed(1)
a=random.randint(1,100)
random.seed(1)
b=random.randint(1,100)

questions.prompt: Expert: do not use random for:

intro.title: datetime module
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Work with dates and times: date, time, datetime, timedelta.

strftime formats; strptime parses. Time zones need zoneinfo (3.9+).

Store UTC in systems; convert for display.
intro.sandboxCode:
from datetime import date, timedelta
today = date.today()
print(today, today + timedelta(days=7))

validation.setupCode:
from datetime import date
ok=date.today() is not None

from datetime import date,timedelta
d=date(2020,1,1)+timedelta(days=7)
ok=d.day==8

challenges.title: Medium: datetime construct
challenges.starterCode:
from datetime import datetime
dt=datetime(2024,6,1,12,30)
ok=dt.year==2024

challenges.title: Expert: isoformat
challenges.starterCode:
from datetime import date
ok="2024" in date(2024,1,1).isoformat()

intro.title: Creating modules
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Any .py file is a module. Save functions in `mymath.py` and `import mymath`.

Module name is filename without .py. Package folders need __init__.py (optional in 3.3+ namespace).

Keep modules focused — one clear responsibility per file.
intro.sandboxCode:
# mymath.py
# def add(a, b): return a + b

# main.py
# import mymath
# print(mymath.add(1, 2))

challenges.title: Expert: split utils
Expert: avoid circular

validation.setupCode: names = [n for n in ["add", "_hidden"] if not n.startswith("_")]

tests.label: Checks names
tests.assertion: "add" in names and "_hidden" not in names

intro.title: Packages
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Packages are directories of modules with __init__.py (can be empty). Import `package.module`.

Organize large projects hierarchically. __init__ can expose public API.

Relative imports use dots inside packages: `from . import sibling`.
intro.sandboxCode:
# pkg/__init__.py
# pkg/utils.py
# from pkg import utils

validation.setupCode:
pkg_modules=["pkg.math","pkg.utils"]
count=len(set(m.split(".")[0] for m in pkg_modules))

intro.title: pip and virtual environments
intro.bodyMarkdown:
pip installs third-party packages from PyPI: `pip install requests`.

Virtual environments isolate project dependencies per project.

Activate venv, then pip install; commit requirements.txt not venv folder.
intro.sandboxCode:
# python -m venv .venv
# pip install requests
# pip freeze > requirements.txt

intro.title: __name__ == "__main__"
intro.bodyMarkdown:
When a file runs directly, __name__ is "__main__". When imported, __name__ is module name.

Guard `if __name__ == "__main__":` runs demo/tests only when executed directly.

Standard pattern for scripts that are also importable modules.

validation.setupCode:
direct=False
name="utils" if not direct else "__main__"

challenges.title: Hard: no run on import
Expert: argparse in main
challenges.starterCode:
import argparse
p=argparse.ArgumentParser()
p.add_argument("--n", type=int, default=1)
n=p.parse_args(["--n","1"]).n

challenges.title: Expert: unittest in main

intro.title: Organizing projects
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Structure: src layout, tests/, docs/, requirements.txt, README.md.

Separate concerns: models, views, utils, config. Consistent naming and imports.

As projects grow, packages beat one giant file.

intro.title: Easy project: Greeting module
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Split a tiny greeting program into `greet(name)` and a main block that calls it twice.

Practice functions, imports conceptually, and the `if __name__ == "__main__"` pattern.

challenges.title: Easy: greet function
challenges.promptMarkdown: Define `greet` returning a string with the name.

intro.title: Medium project: Capstone planning
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Combine skills: structure folders, entry script, modules, tests, README.

Plan features, data model, CLI/API boundary, error handling, and persistence first.

Iterate: MVP → polish → extras. Version control and small commits help.
intro.sandboxCode:
"""
Capstone checklist:
- data model
- core functions
- CLI or menu
- persistence
- tests for key logic
"""

def project_score(features_done, tests_done):
    return features_done * 10 + tests_done * 15

print(project_score(4, 2))

intro.title: Hard project: Contact book
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Dict keyed by name or list with search. Fields: name, phone, email.

Commands: add, find, update, delete. Save JSON to disk.

Normalize phone digits for comparison; validate email contains @.
